page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 72 dedicated physical cores and 72 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz with single-threaded cores. It is equipped with 576.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core. The server includes a massive local storage capacity of 45,000 GB utilizing NVMe SSDs, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 112 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across memory and database operations. The server achieves top-tier status in memory bandwidth for both read and write operations, as well as in Passmark memory scores and Redis SET operations. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, as demonstrated by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, single-core performance is average, and small-model LLM text generation is poor, though medium and large model text generation speeds remain strong.

The i8ge.18xlarge offers high resource density, combining substantial memory with exceptionally large local NVMe storage. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-throughput local storage and massive memory bandwidth without the overhead of external storage attachments. The primary tradeoff is the arm64 architecture, which requires compatible software builds, and the average single-core performance. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, high-throughput static web serving, and software compilation pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 72 dedicated physical cores of the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 576.0 GB of memory and a massive 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 112 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and Passmark database operations, alongside strong multi-core CPU performance. While single-core performance is average, the server's high resource density makes it a highly efficient choice for data-intensive workloads. It is best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput static web serving, memory-intensive caching layers, and software compilation pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.18xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 72 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 576 GB RAM, 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier database and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: An ARM-based storage-optimized server featuring massive local NVMe capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 72 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 576.0 GB of system memory and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Delivers 112 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, Redis SET, and database operations
Best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving
